data.title: LIBRA, Solana, and Melania discussions dominate social media buzz
data.description:
Santiment data unveiled six crypto projects with the highest spike in social media discussions compared to their average rates. The LIBRA scandal, MELANIA’s scam FUD, Solana-based memecoins, and the FORT and STORJ tokens were some of the crypto topics dominating social media buzz.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/libra-solana-melania-media-buzz/

data.title: Safe-haven demand drives gold prices higher, but risk of a stronger dollar remains
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Gold futures prices rose due to safe-haven demand. Nikos Zabullas, a senior financial commentator at Tradu, believes that gold has consolidated its position as the premier safe-haven asset and has become an attractive tool to hedge against inflation. Gold prices have risen by nearly 10% so far this year, thanks to President Trump's destructive approach, radical rhetoric, and potential tariffs that could hit global trade and supply chains. In the uncertain "Trump 2.0" era, gold will naturally benefit from risk aversion and central bank purchases. However, if inflation rebounds, the Federal Reserve will adopt more cautious monetary policy easing measures, which could push up the dollar and curb gold demand. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Long/Short Ratio for The SOL/USDT Perpetual Contract is 4.77, Funding Rate at 0.0032%
data.description: According to Binance market data, the long/short ratio for SOL/USDT perpetual contracts is 4.77, with Long dominating. Currently, the funding rate is 0.0032%. The long/short ratio and funding rate are key market sentiment indicators in futures trading.

data.title: QCP: The options market is distorted before the Ethereum Pectra upgrade, and the crypto market is still on the sidelines
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News QCP published an article stating that in the options market, the term structure showed a significant distortion near the March expiration date, especially on ETH, which may reflect the market's positioning before the Ethereum Pectra upgrade-currently under testing and expected to be officially launched in early April.
Looking back at past upgrade events, The Merge (September 2022) followed the classic "buy the rumor, sell the news" pattern-ETH rebounded more than 100% from its June low, but sold after the event. In contrast, the Shanghai upgrade (April 2023), which allowed pledge withdrawals, was viewed pessimistically by the market due to concerns about excess supply. However, once the selling pressure failed to materialize, ETH rose 30% in the following months.
Traders may be preparing for another volatility event as the relevant narrative has gained traction, and volatility has favored call options since March 28-this may set the stage for the next positioning theme as the crypto market remains on the sidelines after the impact of Trump's tariff issues.
A countervailing force is broad weakness in altcoins — LIBRA’s collapse, SOL and ETH falling back to pre-election levels, and BTC’s market dominance near all-time highs. In addition to market catalysts, a sustained recovery in altcoins will likely require real real-world adoption and network development progress, not just speculative capital flows.

data.title: Ethereum client Geth calls on validators to upgrade immediately to prevent potential financial losses
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The developers of the Ethereum client Geth called on validators using version v1.15.1 to upgrade to v1.15.2 immediately to prevent potential financial losses. The upgrade aims to maintain network stability and prevent interference with validator operations. There is a regression issue in version v1.15.1, which may cause block creation on the Ethereum mainnet to fail. Validators running Geth v1.15.1 may miss block opportunities, resulting in loss of block rewards and transaction fees. This upgrade follows a recent fix patch. Previously, there was a vulnerability in the old version of Geth that could cause nodes to suffer a denial of service (DoS) attack. (The Block)

data.title: Institution: The Bank of England may accelerate the pace of interest rate cuts in the second half of the year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Luke Bartholomew, a senior economist at investment company Abrdn, said in a report that the impact of the upcoming increase in UK employer national insurance contributions and the national living wage may prompt the Bank of England to accelerate the pace of interest rate cuts in the second half of this year. Data on Tuesday showed that the average wage growth rate rebounded to 5.9% by the end of 2024, and for now, interest rate cuts will still be gradual. However, the biggest test will be how the labor market responds to the changes announced in the government's autumn budget. There are clear signs that expectations for hiring intentions have cooled. If economic growth remains very slow, there is a clear possibility that the Bank of England will cut interest rates faster in the second half of this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum's Beam Chain Project Undergoes Name Change Due To Trademark Issues
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Ethereum core developer Justin.eth Drake announced on Twitter that the Beam Chain project will undergo a name change due to trademark issues associated with the name 'Beam.' During the first community conference call, it was revealed that eight new teams have joined the project, with support from six mainnet consensus layer teams. Additionally, two new coordinators have been introduced to the Ethereum Foundation.

Currently, the Beam Chain project comprises 14 consensus layer teams, representing every continent except Antarctica. These teams utilize a variety of programming languages, including Zig, C, C++, and C#. The project has also seen the emergence of several self-funded teams.

data.title: UK Interest Rate Cuts May Accelerate Amid Economic Changes
data.description: According to Odaily, Luke Bartholomew, a senior economist at investment firm Abrdn, has indicated in a report that upcoming increases in UK employer national insurance contributions and the national living wage could prompt the Bank of England to accelerate interest rate cuts in the latter half of this year. Recent data from Tuesday shows that the average wage growth rate is expected to rise to 5.9% by the end of 2024, suggesting that interest rate reductions will continue gradually. However, the primary challenge will be how the labor market responds to changes announced in the government's autumn budget. There are clear signs that hiring intentions are cooling. If economic growth remains sluggish, the Bank of England may indeed expedite rate cuts later this year.

data.title: DeepSeek launches NSA for ultra-fast long-context training and inference
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: DeepSeek launches NSA. DeepSeek said that NSA is a hardware-consistent and natively trainable sparse attention mechanism for ultra-fast long-context training and reasoning. Through optimized design for modern hardware, NSA speeds up reasoning while reducing pre-training costs without affecting performance. On general benchmarks, long-context tasks, and instruction-based reasoning, its performance is comparable to or even better than the full attention model. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum's Price Drop May Affect Altcoin Season, Analyst Suggests
data.description: According to Odaily, on-chain analyst Ali has stated that if Ethereum (ETH) falls below $2,600, it could hinder the onset of an altcoin season.
